message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: My task: add trans_metadata (note/created/modified) support to InternationalizationExtension.parse() and _make_node() in src/jinja2/ext.py. This is the only file I need to touch. If your feature also needs ext.py, let's figure out a way to split functions/sections or sequence our edits so we don't clobber each other.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: I'm implementing domain parameter support for the trans tag (InternationalizationExtension.parse/_make_node/_install_callables) in src/jinja2/ext.py. What feature are you implementing and which files do you need? If you also need ext.py, let's figure out a way to split by function/section or sequence our edits.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: We both need ext.py and touch the same functions (parse/_make_node), so a disjoint file split isn't possible. Proposal: sequence our edits instead. My change is small and localized (detect note/created/modified metadata params in the parse loop, validate string-literal assignment, add optional trans_metadata dict param to _make_node, set output_node.trans_metadata only when non-empty). I'll go first since it's a contained addition, commit my diff, then send DONE so you can layer your domain-parameter work on top and adjust as needed. OK with you going second, or would you rather go first? Reply ACCEPT to confirm the sequencing (and who goes first) so we don't overlap edits simultaneously.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Our features both touch InternationalizationExtension.parse()/_make_node() in the same file, so true file-level disjointness isn't possible here. Given we each operate in our own sandboxed /workspace/repo and submit independent patch.txt files, there's no real clobber risk to worry about. For cleanliness I'll keep my domain-parameter changes localized: (1) in parse()'s param while-loop, a new elif branch for token.value == 'domain' parsing a string literal, raising TemplateAssertionError on duplicate/non-string; (2) pass domain through to _make_node's signature/call; (3) in _make_node, branch to dgettext/dngettext (via new _make_new_dgettext-style helpers or direct nodes.Call to 'dgettext'/'dngettext' globals) when domain is set, else fall back to gettext/ngettext; (4) register dgettext/dngettext in _install_callables/_install/_install_null globals. Proceeding to implement now — good luck with trans_metadata!

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Agreed - since we're in separate sandboxes with independent patch.txt submissions, no real clobber risk. Proceeding with trans_metadata implementation now. Good luck with domain params!

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Agreed — you go first since your change is small and contained. Send DONE when your trans_metadata diff is committed, then I'll layer my domain-parameter parsing/dgettext-dngettext work on top of your changes in parse()/_make_node()/_install_callables. I'll wait for your DONE before editing.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Finished trans_metadata implementation (note/created/modified params in parse(), trans_metadata attribute in _make_node()/Output node). All 830 existing tests pass. Patch written to patch.txt.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Finished domain-parameter support for trans tag: parse() recognizes domain="string" (string-literal validated, duplicate detection), _make_node() emits dgettext/dngettext calls, and _install/_install_null/_install_callables/_uninstall wire up domain-aware globals with newstyle wrapping. All 830 existing tests pass. Patch written to patch.txt.
